title: @MillieMarconnni: A researcher built a Claude Code skill set that autonomously runs ML experiments...
rawContent:
Tweet by @MillieMarconnni (Millie Marconi)
Author bio: Founder backed by VC, building AI-driven tech without a technical background. In the chaos of a startup pivot- learning, evolving, and embracing change.
Text: A researcher built a Claude Code skill set that autonomously runs ML experiments, rewrites paper narratives, and kills unrepro claims overnight with no human in the loop, and documented a real run that took a paper from borderline reject to submission-ready.

It's called Auto-claude-code-research-in-sleep and the documented overnight run is the most compelling part of the repo. Starting at 5.0/10 with a borderline reject verdict, the system ran 20+ GPU experiments across 4 rounds, found that the main improvement claim didn't survive a large seed study, pivoted the entire narrative to diagnostic evidence that did hold up, and hit 7.5/10 by morning.

The safety constraints are built into the skill design itself. Experiments over 4 GPU hours get skipped. The reviewer is explicitly prompted not to hide weaknesses to game a better score. The loop has to actually implement fixes before it can submit for re-review.

MIT License. 100% Opensource.

(Link in comments)
